name: anythingllm-assistant
description: AI Chat & AI Agent extension for VS Code powered by AnythingLLM. Chat with your documents, upload files, and leverage RAG directly from the editor.

chatParticipants.name: anythingllm
chatParticipants.description: Chat with your AnythingLLM documents & workspaces

commands.name: summarize
commands.description: Summarize the active file or selection

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain the active selection or file

commands.name: search
commands.description: Search relevant documents in the workspace without calling the LLM

commands.name: upload
commands.description: Upload the active file to an AnythingLLM workspace

commands.name: agent
commands.description: Tier 3 — Run an AI Agent: autonomous plan + tool calls for complex goals

disambiguation.description: Questions about information contained in the user's AnythingLLM documents / knowledge base.
examples: Find information about the leave policy in my documents
What's a summary of the documents I just uploaded?
Ask my knowledge base about X
Based on my documents, explain Y

viewsWelcome.contents:
No API key configured yet.
[Configure API Key](command:anythingllm.setApiKey)
Or set the Base URL first:
[Set Base URL](command:anythingllm.setBaseUrl)

Or open the full Chat Panel:
[Open Chat Panel](command:anythingllm.openChatPanel)

No threads yet. Create one or start chatting:
[Open Chat Panel](command:anythingllm.openChatPanel)
[New Thread](command:anythingllm.newThread)

commands.title: AnythingLLM: Upload Active File to Workspace
AnythingLLM: Embed Existing Document into Workspace

anythingllm.baseUrl.description: Base URL of your AnythingLLM API instance (no trailing slash). Example: http://localhost:3001/api

anythingllm.defaultWorkspace.description: Default workspace slug used when the user hasn't picked one.

enumDescriptions: Query: document retrieval only, no LLM
anythingllm.chatMode.description: Default mode when chatting with a workspace.

anythingllm.requestTimeoutMs.description: Request timeout to the AnythingLLM API, in milliseconds.

anythingllm.maxRetries.description: Maximum number of retries on request failure (5xx / network error).

anythingllm.enableTelemetry.description: Enable local telemetry logging (request count, error count, latency). Does not send data to any third party.

anythingllm.showCitations.description: Show source document citations / references under chat responses.

anythingllm.agentMode.description: Enable Tier 3 Agent mode by default. The agent will plan + execute tool calls autonomously for complex goals.

anythingllm.uiTheme.description: Chat Panel theme. Auto = follow VS Code's theme.

anythingllm.uiAccent.description: Accent color for buttons, badges, citations, and the agent plan in the Chat Panel.

anythingllm.agentPlanner.description: Planner strategy for the Agent. react = ReAct loop (Reason → Act → Observe), heuristic = pattern-match, llm = ask the LLM to produce a JSON plan, native = use the model's function-calling API.
enumDescriptions: llm — ask the LLM to produce a JSON plan
native — use the model's native function-calling API

anythingllm.agentMaxIterations.description: Maximum ReAct loop iterations before the agent is forced to synthesize a final answer.

anythingllm.multiTurnContext.description: Send the last 10 messages as context to the LLM (multi-turn conversation).

anythingllm.thinkBlocksCollapsed.description: Auto-collapse AI reasoning (<think> / chain-of-thought). Click to expand.

anythingllm.showStatusBar.description: Show a status bar item (active workspace + agent mode + token count).

anythingllm.costBudgetUsd.description: Daily cost budget in USD. A warning appears when the estimated cost exceeds the budget.

anythingllm.autoStartMcp.description: Auto-start all enabled MCP servers when the extension activates.

anythingllm.mcpServers.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configurations. Each server runs as a child process with stdio JSON-RPC.

walkthroughs.title: Welcome to AnythingLLM for VS Code
walkthroughs.description: Chat with your documents, leverage RAG, and run autonomous AI agents directly from VS Code.

steps.title: 1. Configure API Access
steps.description:
Set your AnythingLLM instance URL and API key.
[Set API Key](command:anythingllm.setApiKey)
[Set Base URL](command:anythingllm.setBaseUrl)

media.markdown:
Configure your **AnythingLLM instance** URL and API key to get started.

- Default Base URL: `http://localhost:3001/api`
- API key is stored securely in VS Code SecretStorage (OS keychain)

Run `AnythingLLM: Set API Key` from the Command Palette.

steps.title: 2. Open Chat Panel
steps.description:
Open the AnythingLLM Chat Panel — your hub for chat, agent, and all features.
[Open Chat Panel](command:anythingllm.openChatPanel)

media.markdown:
Press **Ctrl+Shift+A** (Cmd+Shift+A on Mac) to open the Chat Panel.

The panel includes:
- Workspace selector
- Slash commands (Ask, Summarize, Explain, Search, Upload, Agent)
- Streaming chat with citations
- Comprehensive settings (6 tabs)

steps.title: 3. Try Agent Mode (Tier 3)
steps.description:
Activate Agent Mode for autonomous multi-step task execution.
[Toggle Agent Mode](command:anythingllm.toggleAgentMode)

media.markdown:
Agent Mode runs a **ReAct loop** (Reason → Act → Observe) with 13 tools:

- `vector_search` — search AnythingLLM documents
- `file_read` / `file_write` — manipulate workspace files
- `grep_search` — regex search with ripgrep
- `terminal_exec` — run a shell command (with permission)
- `web_fetch` — fetch a URL as context
- `run_diagnostics` — pull VS Code diagnostics
- `git_status` / `git_diff`
- `mcp_call` — invoke an external MCP tool
- And more

Try: `🤖 Find documents about the leave policy and summarize the key points`

steps.title: 4. Explore All Features
steps.description:
Image upload, export chat, pin messages, MCP integration, and more.
[Open Settings](command:anythingllm.openSettings)

media.markdown:
Other features to explore:

- 🖼️ **Image upload** — drag & drop or paste images
- 💾 **Export chat** — to Markdown or JSON
- 📌 **Pin messages** — important messages survive Clear
- 🗂️ **Workspace Documents** — manage workspace documents
- 🤖 **Agent Run History** — audit log of agent executions
- 🔌 **MCP** — Model Context Protocol for external tools
- 📊 **Token tracker** — token & cost estimation
- 💭 **Collapsible think blocks** — AI reasoning hidden by default, click to expand

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
